name: Microsoft Copilot
tagline: Microsoft's AI assistant for chat, code, docs, and productivity across Windows and the web

overview.description: Microsoft Copilot is Microsoft's flagship AI assistant, powered by OpenAI's GPT-4 and integrated across Windows 11, Microsoft Edge, Microsoft 365, and the web at copilot.microsoft.com. For developers, Copilot offers a versatile chat interface for reasoning through code problems, generating documentation, and exploring APIs — with Bing-powered web search grounding answers in current information. It bridges the gap between coding AI and general productivity AI.
pros: Available for free on the web, in Edge, and in Windows 11 sidebar — no install required
Grounded in Bing search — answers reference current documentation and Stack Overflow
Handles code, writing, diagrams, and image generation (DALL-E) in one interface
Deeply integrated with Microsoft 365 — draft in Word, analyze in Excel, summarize in Teams
Copilot in VS Code (Copilot Chat) gives workspace-aware inline AI assistance
Copilot Studio lets teams build custom Copilot agents for internal workflows
Multimodal — understands screenshots, diagrams, and pasted error images
cons: Less specialized for pure code generation than GitHub Copilot or Claude
Web interface context window is smaller than Claude's for long file pastes
Quality for niche frameworks or libraries varies — always verify answers
Microsoft 365 Copilot features require a paid Business/Enterprise license
Cannot directly read or edit local files without additional integrations
Does not know your private code unless it's visible in the editor window
Does not always understand the full context — you need to provide it explicitly
May hallucinate details if the question is too vague or general
Does not replace engineering thinking — it accelerates, not replaces
whenToUse: Quick code questions without opening a full IDE session
Researching a library or API with up-to-date Bing-backed documentation
Drafting technical documentation, PRs, or ADRs with Word/Teams integration
Debugging by pasting error screenshots — Copilot reads images
Generating quick boilerplate or SQL queries in the browser
Summarizing long GitHub threads, Confluence pages, or Jira tickets
Building internal AI assistants using Copilot Studio

strengths.title: What Copilot Does Especially Well for Developers

areas.title: Rapid Understanding of Existing Code
details: Explains classes, components, services, pipelines, and patterns
Summarizes large files or entire projects
Provides insights on architecture, dependencies, and anti-patterns

areas.title: Fast, High-Quality Code Generation
details: Creates full Angular components (HTML + TS + SCSS + Tests)
Creates Controllers, DTOs, Services, and EF Models in .NET 8
Smart completions that understand the project context

areas.title: High-Level Refactoring
details: Improves naming, structure, and file organization
Converts imperative code to RxJS or async/await
Improves performance, readability, and SOLID compliance

areas.title: Test Writing
details: Suggests mocks, stubs, and test cases you hadn't thought of

lesserKnownCapabilities.title: Capabilities Most People Don't Know About

steps.title: Use Copilot on the web
steps.description: Go to copilot.microsoft.com in any browser. Sign in with your Microsoft account for free access. A Copilot Pro subscription unlocks priority access, GPT-4 Turbo, and longer sessions.

steps.title: Enable Copilot in Windows 11
steps.description: Copilot is built into Windows 11 22H2+. Press Win+C to open the sidebar, or click the Copilot button in the taskbar. It reads your screen context and can act on Windows settings.

steps.title: Activate Copilot in Microsoft Edge
steps.description: Click the Copilot icon (✦) in the Edge toolbar or press Ctrl+Shift+. to open the sidebar. Copilot in Edge can summarize the current page, explain selected text, and compose replies.

steps.title: Install Copilot Chat in VS Code
steps.description: Open Extensions (Ctrl+Shift+X), search for 'GitHub Copilot', install it, sign in with GitHub, and verify the extension is active under File → Preferences → Settings → Copilot → Enable.

tips.title: Set up Custom Instructions (critical first step)
tips.description: This alone improves response quality by 30–40%. Go to Ctrl+Shift+P → Copilot: Open User Settings → Copilot Chat → Custom Instructions, and paste your team's profile.
tips.exampleInstruction: I am a developer on a team working with Angular 20 and .NET 8. Give me clean, modern code with emphasis on SOLID, Clean Architecture, and RxJS Best Practices. In Angular use Signals, Standalone Components, inject(), and new Control Flow. In .NET 8 use Minimal APIs, modern DI, and EF Core 8. When I ask for code — give complete examples. When I ask for an explanation — keep it short and clear. When there are multiple options — suggest 2–3 and explain the differences.

tips.title: Set the tone
tips.description: Add a tone directive to your custom instructions: 'Respond in a professional, direct manner — no filler or fluff.'

tips.title: Activate 'Precise' mode for code tasks
tips.description: In Copilot on the web or Edge, select 'More Precise' conversation style before asking code questions. This reduces creative drift and produces more deterministic, factual code output.

tips.title: Use Copilot in Edge for documentation research
tips.description: Browse to Angular docs, MDN, or the library's GitHub README, then open Edge Copilot sidebar and ask it to summarize or explain the page. It reads the current page as context automatically.

tips.title: Use plugins and Copilot Studio for integrations
tips.description: In Copilot on the web, enable plugins (GitHub, Jira, Confluence) to let Copilot pull live data from your tools. For team-specific workflows, Copilot Studio lets you build custom agents with your own data.

tips.title: Upload screenshots for visual debugging
tips.description: Copilot is multimodal. Paste or drag in a screenshot of an error message, UI bug, or network trace. Ask 'what is causing this?' and it will analyze the image as context.

tips.title: Provide clear context
tips.description: Specify the goal, constraints, and technology stack. Paste a code snippet instead of asking a vague question. Ask for alternatives. Ask for a short explanation. Ask for improvements, not just 'write this for me'.

tips.title: Write clean code for better completions
tips.description: Well-structured code leads to better inline suggestions. The more context in the file, the more accurate Copilot becomes. Write incrementally — don't paste 300 lines at once.

tips.title: Paste errors as images, not just text
tips.description: When debugging, take a screenshot of the full terminal/browser error (including surrounding context) and paste it directly into Copilot. Visual context often leads to faster and more accurate diagnosis than text alone.

tips.title: Use it as a first-pass research tool
tips.description: Before diving deep into docs, ask Copilot to summarize how a library or concept works. Its Bing grounding keeps answers current. Then verify critical implementation details against the official docs.

tips.title: Combine with Microsoft 365 for documentation
tips.description: Use Copilot in Word to draft technical specs from your notes, in Excel to analyze data or generate formula explanations, and in Teams to summarize meeting discussions for your dev log.

tips.title: Be explicit about your tech stack
tips.description: Copilot's general knowledge spans many stacks. Always specify yours: 'Using Angular 21, standalone components, signals, Tailwind CSS 4'. Without this context, it may default to older patterns.

tips.title: Use it for cross-cutting concerns
tips.description: Copilot excels at tasks that span multiple tools — like 'summarize this Jira ticket, generate a branch name, and draft the PR description'. Use it to connect dots across your entire workflow, not just for coding.

gitlabWorkflow.title: Working with Copilot in a GitLab Environment (Merge Requests)
gitlabWorkflow.description: Even though Copilot is not built into GitLab, you can still use it to analyze MRs by copying the diff or changed files into Copilot Chat.
examplePrompts: @workspace Analyze the following diff and suggest improvements.
Summarize this MR and highlight areas for improvement.

prompts.title: Debug from Screenshot
prompts.description: Paste an error screenshot and get a diagnostic with root cause and fix.
prompts.content:
[Paste or drag your error screenshot here]

This error appears in my [Angular / Node / browser] application.

Please:
1. Identify the root cause based on what you see
2. Explain why this error occurs
3. Provide a concrete fix
4. Suggest how to prevent this class of error going forward

Context: I am using [Angular 21, TypeScript 5.x, Node 20]

prompts.title: Summarize a Library or API
prompts.description: Get a developer-focused summary of a library, API page, or GitHub README.
prompts.content:
[Open the library docs or GitHub README in Edge, then open Copilot sidebar]

Summarize this library for a senior frontend developer:
1. What problem does it solve?
2. Core concepts and API surface (key classes / functions / directives)
3. When should I use this over alternatives?
4. Minimal working example for the most common use case
5. Known gotchas or limitations I should know about

prompts.title: Draft a PR Description
prompts.description: Generate a clear, structured pull request description from your change summary.
prompts.content:
Write a pull request description for the following change:

What changed:
[describe the change in plain language]

Why:
[describe the motivation or linked ticket]

Format it as:
## Summary
[2–3 bullet points]

## Changes
[bulleted list of what changed]

## Test Plan
[checklist of how to verify the change works]

## Notes
[any rollback plan, migration steps, or reviewer notes]

prompts.title: Write a SQL Query
prompts.description: Generate or explain a SQL query with plain-language requirements.
prompts.content:
Write a SQL query for the following requirement:

[Describe what data you need in plain English]

Schema:
```sql
[paste your table definitions here]
```

Requirements:
- Return only the columns I need (no SELECT *)
- Handle NULLs safely
- Use CTEs if the query is complex
- Add a brief comment explaining each step
- Target database: [PostgreSQL / MySQL / SQL Server]

prompts.title: Extract Action Items from Meeting Notes
prompts.description: Turn messy meeting notes into a clean action item list for your team.
prompts.content:
Extract all action items from the following meeting notes and format them as a table:

[paste your raw meeting notes here]

Table format:
| Action Item | Owner | Due Date | Priority |

After the table, list any open questions or decisions that still need to be made.

prompts.title: Explain This Code
prompts.description: Get a plain-English explanation of a complex code snippet.
prompts.content:
Explain the following code to a developer who is familiar with the language but new to this pattern:

```
[paste your code here]
```

Your explanation should:
1. Describe what this code does in plain English (no jargon)
2. Explain each non-obvious step
3. Identify the design pattern being used (if any)
4. Point out anything that could be improved or might cause issues
5. Suggest 1–2 resources for learning more about the core concepts

prompts.title: Explain and Improve a File
prompts.description: Ask Copilot to explain a file's purpose and suggest improvements.
prompts.content: @file Explain what this file does and what could be improved.

prompts.title: Improve Angular Code to Best Practices
prompts.description: Refactor current file code to follow Angular 20 best practices.
prompts.content: @file Improve this code according to Angular 20 best practices.

prompts.title: Create Angular Standalone Component
prompts.description: Generate a standalone Angular component with Signals, inject(), and new Control Flow.
prompts.content: Create a Standalone Angular component with Signals, inject(), and new Control Flow syntax.

prompts.title: Convert to Clean RxJS
prompts.description: Refactor imperative code into clean RxJS reactive patterns.
prompts.content:
Convert the following code to clean RxJS:

```
[paste your code here]
```

prompts.title: Create Angular Service with HttpClient
prompts.description: Generate an Angular service with HttpClient and an error-handling interceptor.
prompts.content: Create an Angular service using HttpClient with an interceptor for error handling.

prompts.title: Create .NET 8 Minimal API
prompts.description: Generate a Minimal API endpoint with EF Core 8, DTOs, and validation.
prompts.content: Create a Minimal API in .NET 8 with EF Core 8 including DTOs and Validation.

prompts.title: Improve .NET Class for SOLID
prompts.description: Refactor a .NET class to follow SOLID principles.
prompts.content:
Improve the following class according to SOLID principles:

```csharp
[paste your class here]
```

prompts.title: Create .NET Integration Tests
prompts.description: Generate integration tests for a .NET API.
prompts.content:
Create integration tests for the following API:

```csharp
[paste your API code here]
```

prompts.title: Write Unit Tests for Current File
prompts.description: Generate unit tests for the current file using Copilot's @tests context.
prompts.content: @tests Write unit tests for this file.

prompts.title: Create GitHub Action for Build + Tests
prompts.description: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for building and testing Angular and .NET.
prompts.content: Write a GitHub Action that runs build + tests for an Angular frontend and a .NET 8 backend.

prompts.title: Create Dockerfile
prompts.description: Generate a Dockerfile for your project.
prompts.content:
Create a Dockerfile for this project.

Context: [Angular frontend / .NET 8 backend / full-stack monorepo]

prompts.title: Create Kubernetes Manifest
prompts.description: Generate a Kubernetes deployment manifest for a service.
prompts.content:
Write a Kubernetes manifest for this service.

Context: [describe the service, ports, replicas, environment variables]

prompts.title: Analyze a Merge Request / Diff
prompts.description: Paste a diff into Copilot Chat to get a summary, bug detection, and improvement suggestions.
prompts.content:
@workspace Analyze the following diff and suggest improvements:

```diff
[paste your diff here]
```

Please:
1. Summarize the changes
2. Identify potential bugs or issues
3. Suggest refactoring opportunities
4. Recommend any missing tests

prompts.title: Generate a Mermaid Diagram
prompts.description: Create a Mermaid diagram (sequence, class, or flowchart) from a description.
prompts.content:
Create a [sequence / class / flowchart] Mermaid diagram for the following:

[describe the flow, classes, or process]
